questions.question: What does a plain function represent?
questions.explanation: A plain function represents a one-to-many correspondence

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a one-to-one function?
questions.explanation: A one-to-one function is a function where every input corresponds to exactly one output

questions.question: What is the condition for a function to be one-to-one?
options: A) The function must be injective
B) The function must be surjective
C) The function must be bijective
D) The function must be constant
questions.explanation: A function is one-to-one if it is injective, meaning it must map distinct inputs to distinct outputs

questions.question: Can a function be both plain and one-to-one?
questions.explanation: Yes, a function can be both plain and one-to-one, as long as it is injective

questions.question: What is an example of a relation that is not a function?
questions.explanation: A relation is not a function if it has more than one output for a given input
